upstream_gate.ordering_evidence: S21 immediately precedes S22 in T1-foundation serial board order

test_commands: AFTER revised implementation and fresh Captain PROCEED; explicit one-call diagnostic, repeatable only by another human invocation: bin/sworn llm-check --proof-receipt --driver-diagnostic --type spec-ambiguity --slice S22-openrouter-tool-structured-output --release 2026-07-15-baton-v0.16-conformance --model <explicit-direct-openrouter-model> --base a09b0e46df465862d00469d4aef2a997442b3d5b

CONDITIONAL ONLY AFTER same-head/model diagnostic PASS, relevant regression fixture, deterministic evidence, full gates, proof bundle, and fresh Captain/Coach authority: bin/sworn llm-check --proof-receipt --certify-driver --type spec-ambiguity --slice S22-openrouter-tool-structured-output --release 2026-07-15-baton-v0.16-conformance --model <diagnostic-pass-model> --base a09b0e46df465862d00469d4aef2a997442b3d5b
reachability_artifacts: cmd/sworn/llmcheck_test.go:TestLLMCheckProofReceiptBinaryReachability drives the built binary through direct OpenRouter configuration into a local fake chat-completions endpoint and asserts the metadata-only receipt, canonical verdict/exit behavior, and no raw output.

receipts/attempt-2.json is the sole credentialed provider reachability artifact: exact v0.16/S22/spec-ambiguity/GLM/start binding, attempt 2, opaque, UNPARSEABLE, exit 2. It is terminal and cannot authorize S20.

effort_complexity.rationale: The material re-scope adds a public native proof mode, atomic metadata durability, narrow error classification, confidentiality controls, exact retry bounds, and a fresh review gate while preserving the direct-only transport boundary.

verification.notes: Planner seeded exact owner-track status blob e9a78deabc640b88f1db913aef1c6c122645f636, preserved attempts 1-3 and the complete maintainability object, then ratified a diagnostic-v1/certification-v3 correction. S22 returns to implementation and fresh design review: explicit one-call diagnostics may repeat across human-selected direct OpenRouter models; certification is one call per relevant changed implementation head after same-head/model diagnostic PASS and full gates. S20 remains blocked until fresh S22 verifier PASS.

release_benefit: Lets an operator troubleshoot the delivered direct OpenRouter driver with repeatable sanitized structural evidence, then certify a changed implementation head separately without weakening the canonical gate, raw-data protections, typed retry policy, or S20's independent verification gate.

design_decisions.choice: Use an explicit direct-only OpenRouter forced-function route for the Coach-selected GLM-5.2 proof model, while retaining every proxy and unprofiled provider as structured-output unsupported.
options: direct-only existing forced-tool route with local canonical validation
treat OpenRouter as an OpenAI strict response-format endpoint
enable all OAI-compatible, proxy, or local endpoints based on URL resemblance
change a default model or canonical report schema
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-17: use OpenRouter with GLM-5.2 for this proof path. The route remains direct-only and does not change a product default.
design_decisions.rationale: The selected model catalogue entry advertises tool support, OpenRouter documents a forced named-function chat-completions contract, and the existing forced-tool driver preserves the canonical schema plus S04's local semantic authority without claiming compatibility for another transport.

design_decisions.choice: Use a native atomic metadata-only receipt and one classified recovery attempt for S22 proof evidence.
options: native pre-dispatch atomic receipt reservation with exactly one retry only for explicit environmental or receipt classes
shell redirection or a generic report field to retain proof evidence
treat the prior missing receipt as a final model verdict
add a broad automatic retry policy or switch provider, model, or transport
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by the Coach, 2026-07-17: record the destroyed-output historical invocation only as attempt 1 receipt_failure/UNPARSEABLE and permit an attempt 2 only under the strict native facility, deterministic evidence, and a fresh Captain design review. No fallback, model default change, generic retry, or S20 activity is authorized.
design_decisions.rationale: A private metadata-only record makes proof state durable without preserving raw provider data. Atomic pre-dispatch reservation prevents an unevidenced dispatch; a narrow allowlist prevents error text, unknown failures, or invalid model output from becoming retry authority.

design_decisions.choice: After immutable attempt 2 ended opaque, authorize one config-only administrative recovery outside the typed retry classifier.
options: resolve verifier.model from standard config with no CLI override, persist only the resolved model ID, and permit exactly terminal attempt 3 after fresh review and gates
leave S22 permanently blocked on the hard-coded GLM proof
silently retry the GLM or choose another model/provider in the command
reset or delete prior receipts and restart the attempt count
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-18: try the proof with the currently configured values through a formal replan. Preserve attempts 1 and 2, make config-only attempt 3 explicit and bounded, and retain no fallback or fourth dispatch.
design_decisions.rationale: The failed proof exposed a policy binding problem rather than a deterministic transport defect. Resolving the customer-selected verifier model through Sworn's single config authority removes the proof-specific hard-code while immutable receipts and a separate administrative gate prevent the exception from broadening normal retry classification.

design_decisions.choice: Separate repeatable driver diagnostics from head-bound certification after immutable attempts 1-3 failed to yield canonical evidence.
options: explicit one-call diagnostic commands across human-selected direct OpenRouter models plus one certification call per relevant changed implementation head
continue consuming proof ordinals as the debugging loop
append replacement slices after already-started blocked S20 and deadlock T1 ordering
choose a model automatically or retain raw provider payloads for debugging
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-18: troubleshooting a driver requires repeated experiments until the cause is fixed. Preserve attempts 1-3, re-scope S22 in place, keep diagnostic invocations explicit and sanitized, and keep certification separate from the debugging loop.
design_decisions.rationale: A strict proof receipt establishes whether certification passed but intentionally lacks the response detail needed to isolate a driver-shape assumption. Enumerated structural diagnostics allow iterative evidence without retaining sensitive payloads; binding certification to a relevant changed head prevents random retry from masquerading as engineering progress.

positive_scenarios: The serial T1 gate preserves S21-openai-structured-envelope verified/PASS from its immutable start before S22 design review, implementation, or proof-receipt dispatch.

A built direct proof-receipt command reaches a local OpenRouter-shaped endpoint, reserves then finalizes one strict metadata-only record bound to the exact release, slice, check, model, and immutable start, and accepts a valid canonical result through the unchanged local gate.
An explicit 429, 5xx, normalized network/deadline, runner, or receipt failure on attempt 1 permits exactly one second dispatch; a valid final result is then terminal.
Historical attempts 1-3 remain byte-for-byte bound to their exact schema, identity and outcomes while diagnostic records use a separate ordinal namespace.

An operator can explicitly run one sanitized diagnostic call, then run later diagnostic commands with the same or another direct OpenRouter model until a synthetic fixture reproduces the structural failure and the driver is fixed.
After same-head/model diagnostic PASS plus full proof and review gates, one certification call writes the next v3 generation and a PASS can proceed to fresh verification.
negative_scenarios: If S21 no longer has its verified/PASS status, S22 remains gated with no implementation or proof-receipt dispatch.

Preflight write failure, a mismatched release/slice/check/model/immutable-start binding, a final prior verdict, invalid receipt path, or exhausted budget makes zero provider requests and cannot consume or reuse S22 retry budget.
HTTP 400/401/402, unknown/local errors, parse/schema/identity/malformed-tool/opaque outcomes, and every historical proof outcome remain non-retryable under the typed classifier.
A diagnostic command without an explicit capable direct OpenRouter model, with stale identity, or with conflicting flags makes zero requests and mutates no prior record.
A failed certification cannot be repeated at the same implementation head; a later generation requires a relevant driver/test change and renewed same-head/model diagnostic and proof authority.

Config paths/content, raw payloads, endpoints, headers, request/response bodies, findings, prompts, diffs, credentials, and keys remain excluded from receipts, normal output, JSON, journals, docs, and Git-visible artifacts; S20 remains blocked until a fresh S22 verifier PASS.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Separating repeatable sanitized structural diagnostics from one-per-changed-head certification will let the operator fix the actual OpenRouter driver incompatibility without deleting history, inventing a default, broadening retry classification, or exposing provider data.

historical_attempt.raw_output_policy: destroyed; do not seek, reproduce, or retain it

terminal_attempt.retry_authority: none under the typed classifier; superseded only by the separately ratified configured-recovery-v2 administrative authority

binding.model_source: config.Load then ResolveVerifierModel with an empty flag; no --model override

may_dispatch_only_after: exact immutable attempt-1 and attempt-2 receipt validation
configured-recovery source implementation and deterministic zero-dispatch/config-only/leak coverage
full S22 suite, go vet, build, and regenerated proof bundle
fresh Captain design review PROCEED and Coach acknowledgement
configured_recovery_attempt.terminal_policy: attempt 3 is immutable and terminal under configured-recovery-v2; it grants no retry authority

driver_diagnostics.model_authority: one explicit human-supplied direct openrouter/ --model per invocation; no config default, fallback, or automatic loop
driver_diagnostics.dispatch_policy: exactly one provider call per valid command; another diagnostic requires another explicit command
driver_diagnostics.evidence_policy: append-only ordinal bound to immutable start and current implementation head; retain only enumerated stage/shape metadata and destroy provider content

eligibility: committed synthetic regression fixture for the diagnosed structural case
targeted deterministic tests, full suite, go vet, build, and regenerated proof
fresh Captain PROCEED and Coach acknowledgement
head_certification.generation_policy: one provider call per relevant changed implementation head; failure is terminal for that head and a later generation requires another relevant driver/test commit plus renewed diagnostics and gates
head_certification.success_policy: only v3 PASS plus fresh artefact-only S22 verifier PASS may unblock S20

non_retryable_outcomes: every attempt-2 and attempt-3 outcome under the typed classifier; diagnostic and certification entry points are separate administrative authority
prohibitions: provider or model call in this Planner session
implicit, config-default, or Sworn-selected model/provider substitution for diagnostics or certification
raw provider data in any artifact or output
automatic model fallback or repeated certification at one implementation head
certification without same-head/model diagnostic PASS and a relevant regression fixture
S20 activity before fresh S22 verifier PASS

captain_review.prior_review: 2cab8cd8 is superseded by the diagnostic-v1/certification-v3 material re-scope

preserved_t1_implementation.note: T1 direct-tool implementation is retained as prior work; the native receipt, retry, and output-sanitization scope remains subject to fresh design review.
